title: picea obovata subsp. tschiketamanica
intro: Picea obovata subsp. tschikotanica, also known as Dahurian spruce, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to eastern Russia and northeastern China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as respiratory problems, fever, and skin infections. The herbal preparations of Dahurian spruce include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Dahurian spruce include its ability to relieve coughs, colds, and flu, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
